purpose: Defines the master agent's state-persistence and recovery systems: master_state.json checkpointing, the post-compaction recovery procedure (Steps 0-5), and the post-swarm metrics.json computation. Loaded by master-protocol when a long-running swarm risks context compaction or after the swarm completes for performance analysis. Informs decisions about how often to checkpoint, what to re-read after compaction, and how to compute parallel_efficiency / max_concurrent for historical comparison.
gotchas: Step 0 of recovery is non-negotiable: re-read role.md BEFORE touching operational state — context compaction frequently evicts the no-code constraint and the master starts implementing fixes itself, the #1 post-compaction failure mode.

Progress files do NOT contain FILES CHANGED data (that comes from worker returns). After compaction, file change data is permanently lost unless mentioned in summary/milestones — note this gap explicitly in downstream UPSTREAM RESULTS.
master_state.json is NOT watched by the server and NOT broadcast via SSE — it is purely for master self-recovery. Workers must never read or write it.
Keep summaries short (one line each) — master_state.json must stay under ~2000 tokens or it bloats every checkpoint write.
Progress files are AUTHORITATIVE if they conflict with the master_state.json checkpoint during recovery — never trust the cached state over ground truth.
metrics.json is write-once during Step 17 finalization — re-writing it corrupts historical comparison.
Printing full status tables during dispatch loops accelerates context consumption and triggers compaction earlier — keep terminal output to one-line confirmations.
patterns: Atomic full-file write for master_state.json after every dispatch/completion/failure event (master is sole writer, no read-modify-write needed).
6-step compaction recovery: re-read role -> read master_state.json -> read initialization.json -> read all progress files -> cross-reference and rebuild upstream cache -> resume eager dispatch.

metrics.json computation: parallel_efficiency = serial_estimate_seconds / elapsed_seconds; elapsed_seconds = max(completed_at) - min(started_at); max_concurrent computed from overlapping started_at/completed_at windows.
On compaction detection, log a 'warn' entry to logs.json: 'Context compaction detected — rebuilt upstream cache from {N} progress files. File change data may be incomplete.'
conventions: master_state.json schema fields: last_updated, completed[], in_progress[], failed[], ready_to_dispatch[], upstream_results{}, next_agent_number, permanently_failed[].
Failed task IDs use 'r' suffix for repair tasks; permanently_failed[] holds IDs that failed twice (original + repair).

metrics.json schema: swarm_name, computed_at, elapsed_seconds, serial_estimate_seconds, parallel_efficiency, duration_distribution{min,avg,max,median}, failure_rate, max_concurrent, deviation_count, total_tasks, completed_tasks, failed_tasks.
Log metrics summary line format: 'Metrics: {N}s elapsed, {X}x efficiency, {N} max concurrent, {X} failure rate'.
